---
title: "Welcome"
description: "What this site is for, and what to expect."
date: "2026-05-11"
categories: [meta]
---

This is the first entry. Mostly to confirm the deploy pipeline works.

## What lives here

- **Long-form notes** on projects I'm building — design decisions, benchmarks, and the reasoning behind the choices.
- **Short posts** on tooling, libraries, and patterns that turned out to be load-bearing.
- **Project pages** that point at the code and summarize what the project is, what worked, and what didn't.

## What doesn't

Polished tutorials, opinion pieces unrelated to engineering, and anything that would be better as a tweet.

## How posts are organized

Each post lives in its own folder under `posts/`, which lets me colocate figures, notebooks, and supporting files with the prose. The site is built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org) and rendered on push by GitHub Actions.

```python
# Code blocks render with syntax highlighting and copy buttons:
def hello(name: str) -> str:
    return f"hello, {name}"
```

And math works too:

$$
\text{score}(q, d) = \sum_{i \in q} \max_{j \in d} \cos(\mathbf{q}_i, \mathbf{d}_j)
$$

If you're reading this, the pipeline is live.
